faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: No rejection/negative-confirm signal is defined in Central→Taxi for the case where no taxi is available for the submitted order (Variant 4b). The driver cannot be informed that the order was rejected and must resubmit.

faults.location: 3.1 Global Definitions and Data structures (Order_struct)
faults.description: The Order_struct has no Priority field, although UC 1.2 Task 2 explicitly requires that orders can be prioritized (some customers are more important than others).

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in Central→Operator to communicate an estimated wait time when an order is very close in time and no car is immediately available (Variant 3b). The operator cannot receive this estimate.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: The LoggedIn(integer) signal carries only the taxi number. UC 1.4 Task 3 requires that both position and driver information are sent to the central upon login. Driver identity is not transmitted.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: No LoginFailed signal is defined in Central→Taxi. UC 1.4 Variant 4b requires the central to reject an invalid card and notify the taxi that login failed. Only LoginOk exists in section 3.4.1.

faults.location: 3.2.2 Signals from the taxi module to the user (taxi driver)
faults.description: No Confirm signal is defined in Taxi→Driver. UC 1.5 Task 4 requires the central's confirmation of order acceptance to be forwarded to the driver, but section 3.2.2 contains no such signal.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: No signal from Central→Taxi notifies the taxi that an order has been automatically cancelled after the 2-minute timeout (Variant 3b). The driver has no way to know the order was cancelled by the system.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: The Position(integer, integer) signal carries only taxi number and zone number. UC 1.6 Task 8 requires that the driver's estimated arrival time is also sent to the central when the taxi is in state 'Soon available'. No time parameter is available.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: No Alarm signal is defined in Taxi→Central. UC 1.7 Task 1 and UC 1.8 Task 1–2 require that the driver pressing the alarm button notifies the central immediately and opens a voice channel. There is no alarm activation signal in section 3.4.2.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: No alarm activation signal (e.g. Start_Alarm) is defined in User→Taxi. UC 1.8 Task 1 requires the driver to be able to trigger an alarm via the terminal, but no corresponding signal is present in section 3.2.1.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: The Position(integer, integer) signal only transmits a zone number (integer), but UC 1.8 Task 3 requires exact coordinates to be sent every 30 seconds during an alarm. Zone-level precision is insufficient for an alarm scenario.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: Reset_Alarm(integer) is defined as a User→Taxi signal (section 3.2.1), meaning the driver can reset the alarm. UC 1.7 Task 3 and UC 1.8 Task 4 explicitly state that only the operator can reset the alarm.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: No ZoneInfo or overview response signal is defined in Central→Taxi. UC 1.9 Task 2 requires the central to send traffic overview information to the taxi terminal upon request, but section 3.4.1 contains no such signal.

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No dispatch reminder signal is defined in Central→Operator. UC 1.10 Task 1 requires the system to notify the operator at an appropriate time before pick-up time for manual dispatch orders.

faults.location: 3.4.2 Signals from the Taxi Module to the Central Module
faults.description: No text message acknowledgment signal is defined in Taxi→Central. UC 1.11 Task 4 requires each receiving car to automatically acknowledge receipt of a text message to the central.

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No text message delivery confirmation signal is defined in Central→Operator. UC 1.11 Task 5 requires the system to confirm delivery of the text message to the operator, and Variants 4b/4c require notification of delivery failure.

faults.use_case: UC 1.12 – Central: Log in to central system
faults.location: 3.3.1 Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: No operator login signal is defined in Operator→Central. UC 1.12 Task 1 requires the operator to submit user identification and password to the system, but no corresponding signal exists in section 3.3.1.

faults.use_case: UC 1.12 – Central: Log in to central system
faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No login confirmation or rejection signal is defined in Central→Operator. UC 1.12 Tasks 2–3 and Variant 2b require the system to confirm successful login or reject incorrect credentials.

faults.location: 3.3.1 Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: The signal Taxi(integer, integer, integer) with parameters (operator number, taxi number, state) appears in section 3.3.1 (Operator→Central), but its semantics describe a response signal returning taxi state information from the system to the operator. It belongs in section 3.3.2 (Central→Operator).

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No signal is defined in Central→Operator for notifying the operator that the requested car is not available or that no connection can be established (Variant 4b).

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in User→Taxi for the driver to request their own properties. UC 1.16 Task 1 requires the driver to be able to initiate a property view from the terminal.

faults.location: 3.4.1 Signals from the Central Module to the Taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in Central→Taxi for sending driver or car property information to the terminal. UC 1.16 Task 2 requires the central to send property information to the car terminal upon request.

faults.location: 3.3.1 Signals from the user (operator) to the central module
faults.description: No signal is defined in Operator→Central for updating driver properties. UC 1.18 Tasks 2–3 require the operator to select a driver and submit updated properties.

faults.location: 3.3.2 Signals from the central module to the user (operator)
faults.description: No acknowledgment signal is defined in Central→Operator for confirming that driver property updates were applied. UC 1.18 Task 4 requires the system to acknowledge the changes.

faults.location: 3.2.1 Signals from the user (taxi driver) to the taxi module
faults.description: No signal is defined in User→Taxi for the driver to mark a text message as read. UC 1.19 Task 3 requires the message to be marked as read when the driver presses the button to read it.

faults.location: 3.6 Communication Protocol Description (Table 1)
faults.description: ZoneInfo is present in Table 1 (message type 16) as a signal transmittable over the radio link, but it is not defined in section 3.4.1 (Central→Taxi signals). The signal is missing from the API definition despite being in the protocol table.

faults.location: 3.6 Communication Protocol Description (Table 1)
faults.description: The Confirm signal (Central→Taxi, section 3.4.1) is not present in Table 1. All signals transmitted over the radio link between Central and Taxi must be coded in the protocol table, but Confirm is absent.

faults.location: 4.1 MSC Order handling, normal case
faults.description: The MSC for order handling omits the Confirm signal from Central→Taxi after the Ack is received (step 8). UC 1.5 Task 4 and UC 1.10 Task 4 require the system to automatically confirm order acceptance to the taxi, but this step is missing from the MSC.

faults.location: 4.1 MSC Order handling, normal case
faults.description: MSC step 1 shows Operator→Central using signal Order(New_Order), but section 3.3.1 defines the operator's order submission signal as OrderOper(Order_struct, integer). The signal name used in the MSC does not match the API definition.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC step 2 shows Central→Comm_link: Start_Voice(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr), but section 3.4.1 defines Start_Voice(integer, integer) with parameters (taxi number, operator number) — the parameter order is reversed relative to the MSC.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC step 5 shows Driver→Taxi: Confirm_Voice(Taxi_Nbr), but section 3.2.1 (User→Taxi signals) does not define a Confirm_Voice signal. The driver has no defined signal to confirm voice communication acceptance.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC step 15 shows Driver→Taxi: Start_Voice(Taxi_Nbr) to indicate the driver pressing the send button to transmit voice, but the correct signal for this action is Send_Voice(integer) as defined in section 3.2.1. Using Start_Voice here is semantically wrong.

faults.location: 4.2 MSC Voice communication, central initiated
faults.description: MSC steps 16–17 show Taxi→Comm_link: Voice_Msg(Operator_Nbr, Taxi_Nbr, Voice_Data) with 3 parameters, but section 3.4.2 defines Voice_msg(integer, integer) with only 2 parameters (taxi number and voice data). The MSC and signal definition are inconsistent in parameter count.
